explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text discussing contested outcomes, formal judgments, or procedural conflict** (such as legal rulings, election results, sports seeding debates, award considerations, or disputed allegations).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across news, legal reports, sports commentary, and political analysis in multiple languages (English, Spanish, French, Italian, Russian, Hindi). It activates on text describing situations where an official decision (by a court, committee, poll, award body, or similar authority) is being reported, debated, or challenged.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The high-activation texts consistently feature a **dispute, evaluation, or formal resolution process** ("deserving of consideration for a 1 seed," "denied the allegations," "lost his case," "win over voters"). The low-activation examples are primarily straightforward reports on events, descriptions, or announcements without this layer of contention or evaluative judgment ("theme parks worldwide shut down," "volcano could be ready to blow," "recensione del film").

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects semantic contexts where a subjective or authoritative judgment is being applied or contested. It focuses on the linguistic framing of procedural fairness, merit, and disputed conclusions across diverse topics.

results.actual_text: jda. Tesla har en trogen kundkrets och åtminstone en del av kvalitetsproblemen som drabbade både Model
results.plain_text: Nöjdaste bilägarna: Tesla kör över konkurrenterna – Volvo hamnar under snittet  Men trots det verkar ägarna vara riktigt nöjda. Tesla har en trogen kundkrets och åtminstone en del av kvalitetsproblemen som drabbade både Model S- och Model X-ägarna i början av tillverkningsfasen för respektive modell är lösta vid det här laget, antingen med garantiåtgärder eller mjukvaruuppdateringar.  Consumer Reports har släppt en ny lista, den här gången över bilmärkena med nöjdast ägare. Här visar det sig att Teslaägarna är nöjdast av alla bilägare i undersökningen och att hela 91 procent skulle göra om sitt bilköp. Tvåa är Porsche där "bara" 84 procent skulle göra om köpet – ett mycket bra resultat i sig självt.  Volvo hamnar på en föga smickrande tjugondeplats av totalt 29 bilmärken. Allra sämst är Fiat där bara 53 procent skulle göra om köpet.  300.000 bilar av modellår 2014–2017 fanns med i undersökningen och det krävs minst två modeller för att ett märke ska kunna delta.  Även bland svenska bilägare ligger Tesla riktigt bra till: hela 97 procent säger att de skulle göra om sitt bilköp i auto motor & sports begagnatenkät. Det är betydligt bättre än snittsiffran på 88 procent.  Bland de svenska Volvoägarna skulle 91 procent göra om köpet. Totalt finns 4.858 betygsatta bilar av modellår 2014–2017.  Om du gillade den här artikeln – skriv gärna upp dig för vårt uppskattade nyhetsbrev. Tack för ditt stöd!  Relaterade artiklar

results.actual_text: VANEK SMITH: A gateway plastic. And they added this catchy name - stop sucking. The campaign went viral. A lot of celebrities got onboard, including
results.plain_text: How The Campaign To Ban Plastic Straws Got Its Start  ARI SHAPIRO, HOST: Starbucks, Ikea, Alaska Airlines, the city of Vancouver, Scotland - they're all banning plastic straws citing environmental concerns. It's starting to feel like a global movement. So why did this environmental campaign take off the way it did, and will it stick? Here's Stacey Vanek Smith of NPR's daily business podcast The Indicator. STACEY VANEK SMITH, BYLINE: Plastic is a huge problem in our oceans. But straws are a relatively minor part of that problem. They represent just a tiny fraction of our total plastic waste. Dune Ives is the executive director of Lonely Whale, the environmental group that spearheaded the straw war in the United States last year. DUNE IVES: When we looked at the plastic straw itself, we realized it's the one thing that connects us every single day to the plastic crisis. So that's where as an organization we landed on the straw. VANEK SMITH: Lonely Whale was struggling with this thing environmental groups often struggle with - the scope of the problem is enormous. Plastic is in everything. And getting people to act can be really hard because the whole thing kind of seems hopeless. But the straw had a few things going for it. First, straws are everywhere. We use millions of them every day. Second, people tend to use straws in public, so giving them up is like a public statement. And third, we don't really need them. IVES: It's the one thing where there's an easy-to-implement solution. So let's start with the straw as a gateway plastic. VANEK SMITH: A gateway plastic. And they added this catchy name - stop sucking. The campaign went viral. A lot of celebrities got onboard, including Ellen Pompeo, aka Meredith Grey from "Grey's Anatomy." (SOUNDBITE OF ARCHIVED RECORDING) ELLEN POMPEO: I accept your challenge, and I vow never to suck again. So who can I challenge? My other favorite Ellen. Ellen DeGeneres, will you stop sucking? VANEK SMITH: So is this a start? First straws, and then other kinds of plastic polluting our oceans? HEATHER BARNES TRUELOVE: Hello, my name is Heather Barnes Truelove. I'm an associate professor of psychology at University of North Florida. VANEK SMITH: Are you a doctor? TRUELOVE: I am. VANEK SMITH: So you're Dr. Truelove? TRUELOVE: Yes. VANEK SMITH: That's the best thing I've ever heard. That's so awesome. Dr. Truelove says tackling a giant problem like plastic in oceans with a small start like straws requires something called positive spillover. You refuse a straw, and suddenly you're kind of identifying yourself as an environmentalist, like, yeah, I'm part of the solution. And she uses the example of a person's health kick. Like, let's say you decide to get really fit, and you join a gym. And then that night, you go out to eat. And the waiter comes by with the dessert cart. And on it is this delicious piece of cake. TRUELOVE: And I say, no. You know, I can't have this cake. I'm on an exercise kick, right? VANEK SMITH: But things can go a very different way when the dessert cart comes around. And that is because of something called moral licensing. TRUELOVE: You say, oh, yeah, you know I exercised today. I can totally get the dessert, right? This sort of - the exercise, the good deed that you've done, has licensed you to do sort of a bad thing in eating the cake. VANEK SMITH: Dr. Truelove says she and other researchers are working to pinpoint what makes us go in a positive spillover direction versus what makes us go in the moral licensing direction. She says it's really subtle. We all have both impulses within us. But she does say that in cases like the straw, humans do have a slight tendency toward positive spillover. She says we do know momentum is key. For the war on straws to have a truly big impact, it will have to spread - first the lids, then the stirrers, then they're coming for your cup. Stacey Vanek Smith, NPR News. Transcript provided by NPR, Copyright NPR.

results.actual_text: , could have done a better job if the Seminoles were the fourth seed in the College Football Playoff over the Crimson Tide. It's not only that some
results.plain_text: The Michigan Wolverines defeated the Alabama Crimson Tide in the Rose Bowl to advance to the national championship game. However, people believe FSU quarterback Tate Rodemaker could have had a better performance than what Alabama's Jalen Milroe was able to finish with.  Milroe finished the game going 16-of-23 for 116 passing yards in the Orange Bowl game against Georgia. Social media was on fire with the thought that the current transfer portal quarterback from the Florida State Seminoles during the season, Tate Rodemaker, could have done a better job if the Seminoles were the fourth seed in the College Football Playoff over the Crimson Tide.  It's not only that some people are saying Tate Rodemaker would have been better than Jalen Milroe, but some people are lumping in J.J. McCarthy as well.  People also pointed out how the College Football Playoff selection committee and the media have disrespected Rodemaker.  While we may never know what the result would have been if we switched Jalen Milroe for Tate Rodemaker, it definitely sparked a lot of conversation.  How well did Tate Rodemaker do for the Florida State Seminoles after replacing Jordan Travis?  Tate Rodemaker was the second-string quarterback behind Jordan Travis. However, once Travis suffered a leg injury and was not going to return to the program, Rodemaker could get some action and show what he could do.  Junior quarterback Tate Rodemaker did an excellent job throughout the season as he went 32-of-56 (57.1 completion percentage) for 510 yards with five passing touchdowns and did not throw an interception. However, in two starts during the regular season, he combined to go 25-of-48 (52.1 completion percentage) for 351 yards with two passing touchdowns.  He did a good job but did not play in either the Atlantic Coast Conference Championship Game nor the Orange Bowl as he was injured for the first game and opted out of the Orange Bowl.  While he did not face the Michigan Wolverines during his two-game stretch as a starting quarterback, he did throw for 134 passing yards against the Florida Gators. This Gators team is not comparable to the Michigan Wolverines' defense at any point throughout the season, so it is difficult to envision Rodemaker doing any better than Milroe did.

results.actual_text: La selección de fútbol de Egipto, se ha quedado
results.plain_text: La selección de fútbol de Egipto, se ha quedado a las puertas de conseguir la Copa África frente a Camerún (1-2). El seleccionador egipcio, Hector Cúper, suma con esta su sexta final sin conocer la victoria y de esta manera prolonga su mala racha en finales a partido único. Finales perdidas: 1. Subcampeón de Recopa El de Chabás consiguió meter al Mallorca en la Recopa de Europa tras disputar la final de la Copa del Rey. En esta ocasión, el conjunto mallorquín llegó a la final de esta competición en la que se enfrentó a la Lazio, en la última edición de esta. 2. Subcampeón de la Champions League, en dos ocasiones Sus logros en el Mallorca no pasaron desapercibidos y el Valencia se fijó en él. En su etapa como técnico valencianista, entre la temporada de 1999 y 2001, consiguió meter al conjunto 'ché' en dos finale seguidas de la Champions. La primera, que fue en la edición del 2000, la perdió contra el Real Madrid en París, y la segunda, en 2001, frente al Bayern de Múnich en San Siro. 3. Subcampeón de la Copa del Rey Tras su paso por el fútbol argentino como entrenador, Cúper arribó en Mallorca y consiguió meter al equipo de las Islas Baleares en la final de la Copa del Rey de 2005, que acabó perdiendo contra el Barcelona en la tanda de penaltis. 4. Subcampeón de la Copa de Grecia En la temporada 2010 se fue a Grecia donde dirigió al Aris Thessaloniki FC, con el que disputó la final de Copa de Greacia frente al PAE Panathinaikos, y aguantando otro año más al cargo del banquillo griego que abandonó por cosechar malos resultados. 5. Subcampeón CAN En la edición de 2017 de Copa África, el preparador argentino, consiguió meter en la final a la selección de Egipto que se enfrentaba a Camerún, llegando a adelantarse en el marcador pero en la segunda parte el conjunto egipcio dejó escapar el partido, haciendo que los 'leones' se alzaran con el título continental. Además, Héctor Cúper ha extendido su gafe a la Liga italiana y argentina, ambas perdidas en la última jornada: Subcampeón en el torneo de Clausura El técnico argentino, que se hizo al frente del Huracán durante la competición del Apertura de 1933, consiguió llegar al final del Clausura de 1994 como principal candidato a conseguir el título, aunque terminó perdiéndolo frente a Independiente. Subcampeón de la Serie A Sus buenas campañas con los equipos españoles hicieron que el Inter de Milán se hiciera con sus servicios en 2001. Estuvo enfrente del banquillo italiano durante dos temporadas, dejando al equipo en la tercera y segunda posición. Fue destituido al comienzo de su tercera temporada por los malos resultados.

results.actual_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the
results.plain_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the years, but Thursdays have a storied history of their own. Author Michael Hurd will take part in a live virtual event for the Allen Public Library to discuss the largely unknown history of Black high school football in the state. Hurd’s Thursday Night Lights: The Story of Black High School Football in Texas was named one of the 30 best Texas books for the 2010-19 decade by The Texas Observer and one of the 30 most essential books about Texas by the Houston Chronicle. The live event will be at 7:30 p.m. May 13 at allentx.swagit.com. Former Dallas Morning News columnist James Ragland will moderate the program. Former Dallas Cowboys receiver Tony Hill will also take part. Thursday Night Lights tells the inspiring story of Black high school football in Texas from 1920 to 1970. The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as “Mean” Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles “Bubba” Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career. Hurd is also the author of Black College Football, 1892-1992, the only book that comprehensively documents the legacies of football programs at historically Black colleges, and he serves on the selection committee for the Black College Football Hall of Fame. He is a board member for the Writers’ League of Texas, a trustee with the Friends of the Texas Historical Commission and a member of the Texas Institute of Letters. For details about the virtual event, call 214-509-4911.
